message.content:
You are a DIRTY-ROOM integrator for the tamarin-rs relicensing campaign, working in /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs. Read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md and the relevant INTEGRATION_REPORT.md sections (the wave-2 sections document each blocker this round aimed to close).
RULES: adapters and extraction only — NEVER transplant ported logic into clean (headerless) files; clean vendored sources get mechanical fixes only. A clean file acquiring a GPL header from scripts/gen_license_headers.py is a provenance violation: stop and report. If a gap still blocks, KEEP the ported path + report precisely. Comments describe current code only. No git commits. Append a dated section to INTEGRATION_REPORT.md; return a plain-text summary.
FINAL VALIDATION: cargo build --workspace; cargo test -p tamarin-parser -p tamarin-theory -p tamarin-prover -p tamarin-server; wellformedness fixture suite; scripts/gen_license_headers.py then --check (0 stale). Report the header-count delta and author citations that disappeared. If you must stop partway, leave the tree BUILDING AND GREEN and report the precise remaining steps.

Integrate units E (macros) and D (console). Audits passed this round for: E, B, A, D — only integrate passed units.
- E (if passed): re-sync crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs from the round-6 workspace (the bare-nullary Var arm now guards on decoration — the exact gap that failed bare_nullary_macro_name_expands last round). Route macro_expand::expand_theory_macros / macro_expanded_clone through macros::expand_staged. Gate: ALL nine macro_expand captured tests pass UNMODIFIED (especially bare_nullary_macro_name_expands, acc_lemma/case_test non-expansion), theory suite green, wf pipeline fixtures green (macro_expanded_clone feeds the wf checks). When green, DELETE the ported expand_theory_macros/expand_items/expand_rule from macro_expand.rs (keep whatever thin staging glue remains necessary, headered as-is).
- D (if passed): finish the coupled swap the wave-2 D agent scoped (its INTEGRATION_REPORT.md section lists the four blockers with live evidence). Re-sync the round-5 clean modules (summary additions + incremental emission API). Then make the run-driver contract changes in the PORTED files (allowed — they stay headered): defer value-validation forcing until after the maude preamble exactly as the reference does; route structural-error one-liners bare to stderr and validation envelopes (error + full help) to stdout via the clean errors module; recover the stop-on-trace absent/present distinction via an options re-parse; stream batch output through the clean incremental emitter (progress as produced). Gates: cli_e2e; tests/console_split_parity.rs; live split-stream comparisons via the console cluster oracle on: --bound=x (preamble-then-error), unknown flag (23-byte stderr, empty stdout), no-input envelope, multi-theory batch with wf warnings + lemma results, bounded --prove incomplete-analysis case. When green, DELETE the ported parse_args/validation bodies and ported framing/summary assembly in run.rs; the three Rust-only flags keep working through clean typed args.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).
47

1548	(origin != a temp path) and split the two action `<li>`s out of `PAGE_PREFIX` behind
1549	an origin slot.

1552	lives in `state::TheoryStore` (`BTreeMap<usize, TheoryEntry>` behind a
1553	`parking_lot::Mutex`); `Server<T>` owns its own `BTreeMap<u64, T::Theory>` +
1554	`next_index` counter and dispatches **synchronously** via `&mut self` over
1555	**immutable** `&Self::Theory` producers. Migrating "one namespace" into Server
1556	(the task's step 3) collides with three ported realities:
1557	  1. **Async + offload.** The axum handlers are `async`; heavy proof search is
1558	     offloaded to `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` (`handlers/theory.rs:596`
1559	     autoprove, `:785` autoprove_all) and single-step apply runs inline. A single
1560	     `Arc<Mutex<Server<WebOps>>>` in `AppState` driven by a synchronous
1561	     `Server::dispatch` would have to run under `spawn_blocking` holding that global
1562	     lock **across** Maude boot (~1s) and multi-second autoprove searches — a
1563	     concurrency-semantics change (all requests serialize on one lock) that must be
1564	     re-validated against `routes_autoprove` (6) and `routes_proof_step` (3), not a
1565	     drop-in.
1566	  2. **Lazy Maude proof-state, cached under a `&self` producer.** `main_content`
1567	     for Proof/Rules/Message/Source views needs the materialised `ProofState`
1568	     (Maude handle + precomputed sources), which the store builds **lazily** and
1569	     **caches into `TheoryEntry.proof_state`** via `TheoryStore::ensure_proof_state`
1570	     (state.rs:224-256, boots Maude, double-checked-locks the store). The clean
1571	     `ProverOps::main_content(&self, thy: &Self::Theory, ...)` takes the theory
1572	     **immutably**, so this caching would need interior mutability on
1573	     `TheoryEntry.proof_state` (Mutex/OnceCell) and a `WebOps` carrying `cfg`
1574	     (`maude_path`, `stop_on_trace`) — a structural change to the ported state type.
1575	  3. **Version-fork semantics live in the store.** `apply_method_and_redirect`
1576	     (theory.rs:163-267) does `clone_at_new_idx_forking_proof_state` (fork the tree),
1577	     `apply_at_path`, then computes the redirect via `nextSmartThyPath` over the NEW
1578	     theory. To map onto `ProverOps::apply_method -> Option<(Theory, focus)>` the
1579	     producer must return the forked+stepped `TheoryEntry` **and** the smart-advanced
1580	     focus path — tractable, but it is the fork/smart-advance logic moving wholesale
1581	     behind the callback, i.e. a real refactor of ported proof-tree code, not glue.
1582	
1583	Consequence: a hybrid (Server for the read/proof subset, ported side-paths for the
1584	rest) is expressly disallowed — it forks the version map between the two owners (the
1585	round-4 "inconsistency, not an adapter" defect). So it is all-through-Server or
1586	ported-router-live; A2 makes all-through-Server byte-regress uploaded pages and A3

1588	(`routes.rs`), state (`state.rs`), and handlers (headers untouched). `web_clean`

7	`lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs`, the macro handling threaded through

9	(`Theory/Model/{Rule,Fact,Formula,Restriction}.hs`, `Lemma.hs`,
10	`ClosedTheory.hs`), and `lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser/Macro.hs`.
11	
12	**Findings: none (0).**
13	
14	The core algorithm is materially different, not a mirror:
15	
16	- Architecture. Haskell has NO single whole-theory expansion pass; macro
17	  application is scattered and lazy — `applyMacroInRule`, `applyMacroInFormula`
18	  (via generic `mapAtoms`), `applyMacroInLemma`, `applyMacroInFact`, etc., each
19	  invoked at different pipeline stages (rule closing, proving, wellformedness,
20	  export) over a flat unexpanded `[Macro]` list. The clean unit implements a
21	  single eager `expand(theory) -> theory` (lib.rs:44) that walks the clean AST
22	  once and drops the declarations — the design the SPEC mandates. The traversal
23	  (`expand_item`/`expand_rule`/`expand_formula`/…) is dictated by the vendored
24	  clean AST (ast.rs), so visiting every term-bearing field is forced, not copied.
25	- Term expansion. Haskell `applyMacros` (Macro.hs:40) keeps bodies unexpanded,
26	  matches a funsym by name+arity (`macroToFunSym`), and RE-RUNS `applyMacros` on
27	  the substituted body to expand nested calls. The clean unit instead
28	  pre-expands every body into a DAG-ordered `MacroTable` (`build_table`,
29	  lib.rs:65) keyed by name, then does a single `substitute_term` with NO re-run
30	  (lib.rs:82,141). Different accounting (memoized table + separate substitution
31	  vs. flat-list lazy re-expansion).
32	
33	Shared shape that is filtered out: "expand args, then substitute formals→args
34	into the body" is the only correct eager, capture-avoiding, parallel-substitution
35	order, dictated by the observed macro semantics ([Q7]); the load-bearing
36	structural choice (pre-expand vs. re-run) differs.

1	//! Unit E — `.spthy` macro expansion (clean-room).
2	//!
3	//! Two entry points, differing only in how much of the theory a single call is
4	//! allowed to touch:
5	//!  * [`expand`] — full-close view: every macro use at every use site (including
6	//!    acc-lemma / case-test formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant and
7	//!    left/right diff rule forms) is replaced by its transitively-substituted
8	//!    body.
9	//!  * [`expand_staged`] — the consumer's parse-stage view. Same expansion of
10	//!    ordinary lemmas / restrictions / predicates / processes / the PRIMARY rule
11	//!    form (and bare-nullary uses), but two carve-outs dictated by the consumer's
12	//!    pipeline staging: acc-lemma and case-test formulas are left UNTOUCHED (a
13	//!    later consumer stage owns their expansion [Q41]), and only the primary rule
14	//!    form is rewritten — derived variant / left-right rule forms are not
15	//!    recursed into, matching the parse-stage fact that a rule then exists only
16	//!    in its primary form [Q42].
17	//!
18	//! In both, the `macros:` declarations are retained in place (with their
19	//! original, unexpanded bodies). See ../BEHAVIOR.md for the observed semantics
20	//! this implements; every rule below traces to a `[Qn]` observation.
21	//!
22	//! Summary of the semantics implemented here:
23	//!  * a macro `name(f1..fk) = body`; call `name(a1..ak)` binds `fi := ai` and
24	//!    substitutes **simultaneously** (parallel / capture-avoiding)          [Q7]
25	//!  * a bare, fully-UNDECORATED name equal to a NULLARY macro (untagged sort,
26	//!    no index, no type annotation) is a parenthesis-free use of that macro and
27	//!    resolves to its body; any decoration keeps it an ordinary variable —
28	//!    `~x`/`$x` (sort), `x.1` (index) and `x:msg` (type) — as do names of
29	//!    arity>=1 macros                                       [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35,Q43,Q44]
30	//!  * a formal matches a body variable by **full identity incl. sort**; `~x`
31	//!    and `$x` do NOT match an untagged formal `x` and stay free            [Q27,Q28]
32	//!  * a nullary macro reserves its name against a same-named formal: inside a
33	//!    body the name resolves to the macro, not the formal                   [Q36]
34	//!  * bodies may call only strictly-earlier macros ⇒ the macro dependency
35	//!    graph is a DAG, so expansion always terminates                        [Q8,Q19,Q20]
36	//!  * expansion is transitive: a body's own macro calls are expanded too    [Q9,Q18]
37	//!  * at the AST level a call's arg-count already equals the macro arity
38	//!    (the parser packs/rejects mismatches); `expand` no-ops on a mismatch)  [Q11,Q12,Q15,Q16,Q17]
39	//!  * the `macros:` declaration block is preserved unchanged in the output;
40	//!    the reference retains it in its pretty output and the consuming
41	//!    pipeline requires it in place                                          [Q37]
42	
43	pub mod ast;
44	
45	use std::collections::HashMap;
46	use ast::*;
47	
48	/// Table of macros whose bodies are already fully expanded (macro-free),
49	/// keyed by macro name. Formals are matched against body variables by full
50	/// `VarSpec` identity, so the substitution map is keyed by `VarSpec`.
51	#[derive(Default)]
52	struct MacroTable {
53	    by_name: HashMap<String, (Vec<VarSpec>, Term)>,
54	}
55	
56	impl MacroTable {
57	    fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&(Vec<VarSpec>, Term)> {
58	        self.by_name.get(name)
59	    }
60	}
61	
62	/// How much of the theory one expansion pass is allowed to touch. The two modes
63	/// share the whole term/formula/rule traversal and differ only at the two
64	/// consumer-staging carve-outs (acc-lemma & case-test formulas; derived rule
65	/// forms).
66	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
67	enum Mode {
68	    /// Full-close view: expand every use site, including acc-lemma / case-test
69	    /// formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant / left-right diff rule forms.
70	    FullClose,
71	    /// Consumer's parse-stage view: leave acc-lemma & case-test formulas
72	    /// untouched (a later stage owns them [Q41]) and rewrite only the primary
73	    /// rule form, not derived variant / left-right forms [Q42].
74	    Staged,
75	}
76	
77	/// Full-close macro expansion: expand every macro use at every use site, keeping
78	/// the `macros:` declarations in place.
79	///
80	/// The result, fed back to the oracle, reproduces the reasoning content the
81	/// oracle computes for the original (modulo-AC variants / expanded-formula /
82	/// guarded-formula), which is byte-identical to a hand-inlined equivalent
83	/// (verified by workspace/byteparity.sh and workspace/formula_parity.sh). The
84	/// `macros:` declaration items pass through unchanged (with their original,
85	/// unexpanded bodies): the reference retains the block in its pretty output and
86	/// the consuming pipeline requires it in place [Q37].
87	pub fn expand(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
88	    expand_with(theory, Mode::FullClose)
89	}
90	
91	/// Staged (parse-stage) macro expansion — the entry the consumer's pipeline
92	/// calls.
93	///
94	/// Identical to [`expand`] except for two carve-outs the consumer's staging
95	/// requires, because it invokes this pass before a later stage that owns them:
96	///  * acc-lemma (`... accounts for "..."`) and case-test (`test <name>: "..."`)
97	///    formulas are left byte-identical — untouched — so the later stage can
98	///    expand them itself [Q41];
99	///  * rules exist only in their primary form at this stage, so only the primary
100	///    premises / actions / conclusions / let-block / embedded restrictions are
101	///    rewritten; derived variant and left-right diff rule forms are not recursed
102	///    into [Q42].
103	///
104	/// Everything else — ordinary lemmas, restrictions, predicates, processes,
105	/// equations, the primary rule form, and bare-nullary resolution — is expanded
106	/// exactly as in full close. The `macros:` declarations pass through unchanged
107	/// [Q37].
108	pub fn expand_staged(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
109	    expand_with(theory, Mode::Staged)
110	}
111	
112	fn expand_with(theory: &Theory, mode: Mode) -> Theory {
113	    let table = build_table(theory);
114	    let items = theory.items.iter().map(|it| expand_item(it, &table, mode)).collect();
115	    Theory {
116	        is_diff: theory.is_diff,
117	        name: theory.name.clone(),
118	        configuration: theory.configuration.clone(),
119	        items,
120	    }
121	}
122	
123	/// Build the macro table with fully-expanded (macro-free) bodies.
124	///
125	/// Macros are processed in declaration order; each body is expanded against the
126	/// macros defined strictly before it. Forward/self/mutual references are parse
127	/// errors upstream [Q8,Q19,Q20], so "earlier only" is complete and cannot loop.
128	fn build_table(theory: &Theory) -> MacroTable {
129	    let mut table = MacroTable::default();
130	    for item in &theory.items {
131	        if let TheoryItem::Macros(ms) = item {
132	            for m in ms {
133	                let body = expand_term(&m.body, &table);
134	                table.by_name.insert(m.name.clone(), (m.args.clone(), body));
135	            }
136	        }
137	    }
138	    table
139	}
140	
141	// ---- term-level expansion -------------------------------------------------
142	
143	/// Bottom-up macro expansion of a term. Arguments are expanded first, then a
144	/// macro call is replaced by its body with formals bound to the expanded args.
145	fn expand_term(t: &Term, table: &MacroTable) -> Term {
146	    match t {
147	        // A bare nullary-macro use is the fully-UNDECORATED plain name: a
148	        // NULLARY macro's name written with untagged sort, no index and no type
149	        // annotation resolves to the (already-expanded) body [Q32,Q33]. Every
150	        // decoration blocks resolution and leaves the name an ordinary variable:
151	        //  * a fresh/pub sort (`~konst`/`$konst`)                          [Q34]
152	        //  * an explicit index (`konst.1`, `konst.2`) — the reference rejects an
153	        //    indexed macro name at parse, so an indexed Var must stay a variable
154	        //    here, not resolve                                        [Q43,Q44]
155	        //  * a type annotation (`konst:msg`) — likewise rejected at parse   [Q32]
156	        // The name of an arity>=1 macro never resolves from a bare name    [Q35];
157	        // a nullary macro reserves its name even against a same-named formal[Q36].
158	        Term::Var(v) => match table.get(&v.name) {
159	            Some((formals, body))
160	                if formals.is_empty()
161	                    && v.sort == SortHint::Untagged
162	                    && v.idx == 0
163	                    && v.typ.is_none() =>
164	            {
165	                body.clone()
166	            }
167	            _ => t.clone(),
168	        },
169	
170	        Term::PubLit(_)
171	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
172	        | Term::NatLit(_)
173	        | Term::Number(_)
174	        | Term::NumberOne
175	        | Term::NatOne
176	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
177	
178	        Term::App(name, args) => {
179	            let args: Vec<Term> = args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect();
180	            if let Some((formals, body)) = table.get(name) {
181	                // At the AST level arg-count == arity for a valid parse. If it
182	                // ever differs (defensive), leave the call unexpanded.
183	                if formals.len() == args.len() {
184	                    let subst = build_subst(formals, &args);
185	                    // `body` is already macro-free; a simultaneous substitution
186	                    // of macro-free args therefore yields a macro-free term.
187	                    return substitute_term(body, &subst);
188	                }
189	            }
190	            Term::App(name.clone(), args)
191	        }
192	
193	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
194	            name.clone(),
195	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
196	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
197	        ),
198	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| expand_term(x, table)).collect()),
199	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
200	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
201	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
202	        ),
203	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
204	            *op,
205	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
206	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
207	        ),
208	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(expand_term(a, table))),
209	    }
210	}
211	
212	/// Build the simultaneous substitution `formal_i := arg_i`, keyed by the full
213	/// `VarSpec` of each formal (so sort/idx participate in matching) [Q27,Q28].
214	fn build_subst(formals: &[VarSpec], args: &[Term]) -> HashMap<VarSpec, Term> {
215	    formals
216	        .iter()
217	        .cloned()
218	        .zip(args.iter().cloned())
219	        .collect()
220	}
221	
222	/// Apply a simultaneous substitution to `t`. A `Var` that matches a key is
223	/// replaced by the bound term **without** re-descending into it (parallel
224	/// substitution — no capture) [Q7]. Non-matching variables (incl. differently
225	/// sorted `~x`/`$x`) are left untouched [Q27,Q28].
226	fn substitute_term(t: &Term, subst: &HashMap<VarSpec, Term>) -> Term {
227	    match t {
228	        Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(v) {
229	            Some(bound) => bound.clone(),
230	            None => t.clone(),
231	        },
232	        Term::PubLit(_)
233	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
234	        | Term::NatLit(_)
235	        | Term::Number(_)
236	        | Term::NumberOne
237	        | Term::NatOne
238	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
239	        Term::App(name, args) => Term::App(
240	            name.clone(),
241	            args.iter().map(|a| substitute_term(a, subst)).collect(),
242	        ),
243	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
244	            name.clone(),
245	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
246	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
247	        ),
248	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| substitute_term(x, subst)).collect()),
249	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
250	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
251	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
252	        ),
253	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
254	            *op,
255	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
256	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
257	        ),
258	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst))),
259	    }
260	}
261	
262	// ---- structural recursion through the theory ------------------------------
263	
264	fn expand_fact(f: &Fact, table: &MacroTable) -> Fact {
265	    Fact {
266	        persistent: f.persistent,
267	        name: f.name.clone(),
268	        args: f.args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
269	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
270	    }
271	}
272	
273	fn expand_atom(a: &Atom, table: &MacroTable) -> Atom {
274	    match a {
275	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => Atom::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
276	        Atom::Less(x, y) => Atom::Less(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
277	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => Atom::LessMset(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
278	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => Atom::Subterm(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
279	        Atom::Action(f, t) => Atom::Action(expand_fact(f, table), expand_term(t, table)),
280	        Atom::Last(t) => Atom::Last(expand_term(t, table)),
281	        Atom::Pred(f) => Atom::Pred(expand_fact(f, table)),
282	    }
283	}
284	
285	fn expand_formula(phi: &Formula, table: &MacroTable) -> Formula {
286	    match phi {
287	        Formula::False => Formula::False,
288	        Formula::True => Formula::True,
289	        Formula::Atom(a) => Formula::Atom(expand_atom(a, table)),
290	        Formula::Not(p) => Formula::Not(Box::new(expand_formula(p, table))),
291	        Formula::And(p, q) => Formula::And(
292	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
293	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
294	        ),
295	        Formula::Or(p, q) => Formula::Or(
296	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
297	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
298	        ),
299	        Formula::Implies(p, q) => Formula::Implies(
300	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
301	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
302	        ),
303	        Formula::Iff(p, q) => Formula::Iff(
304	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
305	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
306	        ),
307	        Formula::Forall(vs, p) => {
308	            Formula::Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
309	        }
310	        Formula::Exists(vs, p) => {
311	            Formula::Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
312	        }
313	    }
314	}
315	
316	fn expand_let(b: &LetBinding, table: &MacroTable) -> LetBinding {
317	    // A macro call may appear in the let value [Q23]; the bound pattern `var`
318	    // is a variable/pattern and is expanded too for uniformity.
319	    LetBinding {
320	        var: expand_term(&b.var, table),
321	        value: expand_term(&b.value, table),
322	    }
323	}
324	
325	fn expand_rule(r: &Rule, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> Rule {
326	    Rule {
327	        name: r.name.clone(),
328	        modulo: r.modulo.clone(),
329	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
330	        let_block: r.let_block.iter().map(|b| expand_let(b, table)).collect(),
331	        premises: r.premises.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
332	        actions: r.actions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
333	        conclusions: r.conclusions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
334	        embedded_restrictions: r
335	            .embedded_restrictions
336	            .iter()
337	            .map(|p| expand_formula(p, table))
338	            .collect(),
339	        // Derived rule forms. Full close expands them — the `(modulo AC)` variant
340	        // and left/right diff projections show the expansion [Q3,Q4,Q26]. In the
341	        // staged view a rule exists only in its primary form [Q42], so these are
342	        // carried through untouched.
343	        variants: match mode {
344	            Mode::FullClose => r.variants.iter().map(|v| expand_rule(v, table, mode)).collect(),
345	            Mode::Staged => r.variants.clone(),
346	        },
347	        left_right: match mode {
348	            Mode::FullClose => r.left_right.as_ref().map(|(l, rr)| {
349	                (
350	                    Box::new(expand_rule(l, table, mode)),
351	                    Box::new(expand_rule(rr, table, mode)),
352	                )
353	            }),
354	            Mode::Staged => r.left_right.clone(),
355	        },
356	    }
357	}
358	
359	fn expand_sapic_action(a: &SapicAction, table: &MacroTable) -> SapicAction {
360	    match a {
361	        SapicAction::New(v) => SapicAction::New(v.clone()),
362	        SapicAction::Insert(k, v) => {
363	            SapicAction::Insert(expand_term(k, table), expand_term(v, table))
364	        }
365	        SapicAction::Delete(k) => SapicAction::Delete(expand_term(k, table)),
366	        SapicAction::ChIn { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChIn {
367	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
368	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
369	        },
370	        SapicAction::ChOut { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChOut {
371	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
372	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
373	        },
374	        SapicAction::Lock(t) => SapicAction::Lock(expand_term(t, table)),
375	        SapicAction::Unlock(t) => SapicAction::Unlock(expand_term(t, table)),
376	        SapicAction::Event(f) => SapicAction::Event(expand_fact(f, table)),
377	        SapicAction::Msr { prems, acts, concs, restrictions } => SapicAction::Msr {
378	            prems: prems.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
379	            acts: acts.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
380	            concs: concs.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
381	            restrictions: restrictions.iter().map(|p| expand_formula(p, table)).collect(),
382	        },
383	    }
384	}
385	
386	fn expand_condition(c: &Condition, table: &MacroTable) -> Condition {
387	    match c {
388	        Condition::Eq(x, y) => Condition::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
389	        Condition::Formula(p) => Condition::Formula(expand_formula(p, table)),
390	    }
391	}
392	
393	fn expand_comb(c: &ProcessComb, table: &MacroTable) -> ProcessComb {
394	    match c {
395	        ProcessComb::Parallel => ProcessComb::Parallel,
396	        ProcessComb::Ndc => ProcessComb::Ndc,
397	        ProcessComb::Cond(cond) => ProcessComb::Cond(expand_condition(cond, table)),
398	        ProcessComb::Lookup(t, v) => ProcessComb::Lookup(expand_term(t, table), v.clone()),
399	        ProcessComb::Let { pat, value } => ProcessComb::Let {
400	            pat: expand_term(pat, table),
401	            value: expand_term(value, table),
402	        },
403	    }
404	}
405	
406	fn expand_process(p: &Process, table: &MacroTable) -> Process {
407	    match p {
408	        Process::Null => Process::Null,
409	        Process::Action { action, body } => Process::Action {
410	            action: expand_sapic_action(action, table),
411	            body: Box::new(expand_process(body, table)),
412	        },
413	        Process::Comb { comb, left, right } => Process::Comb {
414	            comb: expand_comb(comb, table),
415	            left: Box::new(expand_process(left, table)),
416	            right: Box::new(expand_process(right, table)),
417	        },
418	        Process::Replication(b) => Process::Replication(Box::new(expand_process(b, table))),
419	        Process::Call { name, args } => Process::Call {
420	            name: name.clone(),
421	            args: args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
422	        },
423	        Process::AtAnnotation(b, t) => {
424	            Process::AtAnnotation(Box::new(expand_process(b, table)), expand_term(t, table))
425	        }
426	    }
427	}
428	
429	fn expand_restriction(r: &Restriction, table: &MacroTable) -> Restriction {
430	    Restriction {
431	        name: r.name.clone(),
432	        formula: expand_formula(&r.formula, table),
433	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
434	    }
435	}
436	
437	fn expand_lemma(l: &Lemma, table: &MacroTable) -> Lemma {
438	    Lemma {
439	        name: l.name.clone(),
440	        modulo: l.modulo.clone(),
441	        attributes: l.attributes.clone(),
442	        trace_quantifier: l.trace_quantifier.clone(),
443	        formula: expand_formula(&l.formula, table),
444	        proof: l.proof.clone(),
445	        plaintext: l.plaintext.clone(),
446	    }
447	}
448	
449	fn expand_predicate(p: &Predicate, table: &MacroTable) -> Predicate {
450	    Predicate {
451	        fact: expand_fact(&p.fact, table),
452	        formula: expand_formula(&p.formula, table),
453	    }
454	}
455	
456	fn expand_item(it: &TheoryItem, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> TheoryItem {
457	    match it {
458	        TheoryItem::Rule(r) => TheoryItem::Rule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
459	        TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => TheoryItem::IntrRule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
460	        TheoryItem::Restriction(r) => TheoryItem::Restriction(expand_restriction(r, table)),
461	        TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(expand_restriction(r, table)),
462	        TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => TheoryItem::Lemma(expand_lemma(l, table)),
463	        // acc-lemma & case-test formulas: full close expands them (the generated
464	        // lemmas' guarded forms show the expansion [Q38,Q39]); the staged view
465	        // leaves them untouched because a later consumer stage owns them [Q41].
466	        TheoryItem::AccLemma(a) => match mode {
467	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::AccLemma(AccLemma {
468	                name: a.name.clone(),
469	                attributes: a.attributes.clone(),
470	                formula: expand_formula(&a.formula, table),
471	                case_test_idents: a.case_test_idents.clone(),
472	            }),
473	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
474	        },
475	        TheoryItem::CaseTest(c) => match mode {
476	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::CaseTest(CaseTest {
477	                name: c.name.clone(),
478	                formula: expand_formula(&c.formula, table),
479	            }),
480	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
481	        },
482	        TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
483	            TheoryItem::Predicates(ps.iter().map(|p| expand_predicate(p, table)).collect())
484	        }
485	        TheoryItem::ProcessDef(pd) => TheoryItem::ProcessDef(ProcessDef {
486	            name: pd.name.clone(),
487	            vars: pd.vars.clone(),
488	            body: expand_process(&pd.body, table),
489	        }),
490	        TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(p) => {
491	            TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(expand_process(p, table))
492	        }
493	        TheoryItem::EquivLemma(a, b) => {
494	            TheoryItem::EquivLemma(expand_process(a, table), expand_process(b, table))
495	        }
496	        TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(p) => {
497	            TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(expand_process(p, table))
498	        }
499	        // Equations carry terms; expand uniformly (a no-op when they contain no
500	        // macro call — not observed in the corpus, but handled for robustness).
501	        TheoryItem::Equations { convergent, eqs } => TheoryItem::Equations {
502	            convergent: *convergent,
503	            eqs: eqs
504	                .iter()
505	                .map(|e| Equation {
506	                    lhs: expand_term(&e.lhs, table),
507	                    rhs: expand_term(&e.rhs, table),
508	                })
509	                .collect(),
510	        },
511	        // Items with no macro-bearing terms are carried through unchanged. The
512	        // `macros:` declaration items pass through here too — retained in place
513	        // with their original, unexpanded bodies [Q37].
514	        TheoryItem::Macros(_)
515	        | TheoryItem::Builtins(_)
516	        | TheoryItem::Functions(_)
517	        | TheoryItem::Options(_)
518	        | TheoryItem::Heuristic(_)
519	        | TheoryItem::Tactic(_)
520	        | TheoryItem::DiffLemma(_)
521	        | TheoryItem::Export { .. }
522	        | TheoryItem::FormalComment { .. }
523	        | TheoryItem::Define(_)
524	        | TheoryItem::Include(_) => it.clone(),
525	    }
526	}

1	//! Unit E — `.spthy` macro expansion (clean-room).
2	//!
3	//! Two entry points, differing only in how much of the theory a single call is
4	//! allowed to touch:
5	//!  * [`expand`] — full-close view: every macro use at every use site (including
6	//!    acc-lemma / case-test formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant and
7	//!    left/right diff rule forms) is replaced by its transitively-substituted
8	//!    body.
9	//!  * [`expand_staged`] — the consumer's parse-stage view. Same expansion of
10	//!    ordinary lemmas / restrictions / predicates / processes / the PRIMARY rule
11	//!    form (and bare-nullary uses), but two carve-outs dictated by the consumer's
12	//!    pipeline staging: acc-lemma and case-test formulas are left UNTOUCHED (a
13	//!    later consumer stage owns their expansion [Q41]), and only the primary rule
14	//!    form is rewritten — derived variant / left-right rule forms are not
15	//!    recursed into, matching the parse-stage fact that a rule then exists only
16	//!    in its primary form [Q42].
17	//!
18	//! In both, the `macros:` declarations are retained in place (with their
19	//! original, unexpanded bodies). See ../BEHAVIOR.md for the observed semantics
20	//! this implements; every rule below traces to a `[Qn]` observation.
21	//!
22	//! Summary of the semantics implemented here:
23	//!  * a macro `name(f1..fk) = body`; call `name(a1..ak)` binds `fi := ai` and
24	//!    substitutes **simultaneously** (parallel / capture-avoiding)          [Q7]
25	//!  * a bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is a parenthesis-free use
26	//!    of that macro and resolves to its body; `~x`/`$x` and names of arity>=1
27	//!    macros are ordinary variables and do not resolve                      [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]
28	//!  * a formal matches a body variable by **full identity incl. sort**; `~x`
29	//!    and `$x` do NOT match an untagged formal `x` and stay free            [Q27,Q28]
30	//!  * a nullary macro reserves its name against a same-named formal: inside a
31	//!    body the name resolves to the macro, not the formal                   [Q36]
32	//!  * bodies may call only strictly-earlier macros ⇒ the macro dependency
33	//!    graph is a DAG, so expansion always terminates                        [Q8,Q19,Q20]
34	//!  * expansion is transitive: a body's own macro calls are expanded too    [Q9,Q18]
35	//!  * at the AST level a call's arg-count already equals the macro arity
36	//!    (the parser packs/rejects mismatches); `expand` no-ops on a mismatch)  [Q11,Q12,Q15,Q16,Q17]
37	//!  * the `macros:` declaration block is preserved unchanged in the output;
38	//!    the reference retains it in its pretty output and the consuming
39	//!    pipeline requires it in place                                          [Q37]
40	
41	
42	use std::collections::HashMap;
43	use tamarin_parser::ast::*;
44	
45	/// Table of macros whose bodies are already fully expanded (macro-free),
46	/// keyed by macro name. Formals are matched against body variables by full
47	/// `VarSpec` identity, so the substitution map is keyed by `VarSpec`.
48	#[derive(Default)]
49	struct MacroTable {
50	    by_name: HashMap<String, (Vec<VarSpec>, Term)>,
51	}
52	
53	impl MacroTable {
54	    fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&(Vec<VarSpec>, Term)> {
55	        self.by_name.get(name)
56	    }
57	}
58	
59	/// How much of the theory one expansion pass is allowed to touch. The two modes
60	/// share the whole term/formula/rule traversal and differ only at the two
61	/// consumer-staging carve-outs (acc-lemma & case-test formulas; derived rule
62	/// forms).
63	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
64	enum Mode {
65	    /// Full-close view: expand every use site, including acc-lemma / case-test
66	    /// formulas and derived `(modulo AC)` variant / left-right diff rule forms.
67	    FullClose,
68	    /// Consumer's parse-stage view: leave acc-lemma & case-test formulas
69	    /// untouched (a later stage owns them [Q41]) and rewrite only the primary
70	    /// rule form, not derived variant / left-right forms [Q42].
71	    Staged,
72	}
73	
74	/// Full-close macro expansion: expand every macro use at every use site, keeping
75	/// the `macros:` declarations in place.
76	///
77	/// The result, fed back to the oracle, reproduces the reasoning content the
78	/// oracle computes for the original (modulo-AC variants / expanded-formula /
79	/// guarded-formula), which is byte-identical to a hand-inlined equivalent
80	/// (verified by workspace/byteparity.sh and workspace/formula_parity.sh). The
81	/// `macros:` declaration items pass through unchanged (with their original,
82	/// unexpanded bodies): the reference retains the block in its pretty output and
83	/// the consuming pipeline requires it in place [Q37].
84	pub fn expand(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
85	    expand_with(theory, Mode::FullClose)
86	}
87	
88	/// Staged (parse-stage) macro expansion — the entry the consumer's pipeline
89	/// calls.
90	///
91	/// Identical to [`expand`] except for two carve-outs the consumer's staging
92	/// requires, because it invokes this pass before a later stage that owns them:
93	///  * acc-lemma (`... accounts for "..."`) and case-test (`test <name>: "..."`)
94	///    formulas are left byte-identical — untouched — so the later stage can
95	///    expand them itself [Q41];
96	///  * rules exist only in their primary form at this stage, so only the primary
97	///    premises / actions / conclusions / let-block / embedded restrictions are
98	///    rewritten; derived variant and left-right diff rule forms are not recursed
99	///    into [Q42].
100	///
101	/// Everything else — ordinary lemmas, restrictions, predicates, processes,
102	/// equations, the primary rule form, and bare-nullary resolution — is expanded
103	/// exactly as in full close. The `macros:` declarations pass through unchanged
104	/// [Q37].
105	pub fn expand_staged(theory: &Theory) -> Theory {
106	    expand_with(theory, Mode::Staged)
107	}
108	
109	fn expand_with(theory: &Theory, mode: Mode) -> Theory {
110	    let table = build_table(theory);
111	    let items = theory.items.iter().map(|it| expand_item(it, &table, mode)).collect();
112	    Theory {
113	        is_diff: theory.is_diff,
114	        name: theory.name.clone(),
115	        configuration: theory.configuration.clone(),
116	        items,
117	    }
118	}
119	
120	/// Build the macro table with fully-expanded (macro-free) bodies.
121	///
122	/// Macros are processed in declaration order; each body is expanded against the
123	/// macros defined strictly before it. Forward/self/mutual references are parse
124	/// errors upstream [Q8,Q19,Q20], so "earlier only" is complete and cannot loop.
125	fn build_table(theory: &Theory) -> MacroTable {
126	    let mut table = MacroTable::default();
127	    for item in &theory.items {
128	        if let TheoryItem::Macros(ms) = item {
129	            for m in ms {
130	                let body = expand_term(&m.body, &table);
131	                table.by_name.insert(m.name.clone(), (m.args.clone(), body));
132	            }
133	        }
134	    }
135	    table
136	}
137	
138	// ---- term-level expansion -------------------------------------------------
139	
140	/// Bottom-up macro expansion of a term. Arguments are expanded first, then a
141	/// macro call is replaced by its body with formals bound to the expanded args.
142	fn expand_term(t: &Term, table: &MacroTable) -> Term {
143	    match t {
144	        // A bare, untagged name equal to a NULLARY macro is that macro used
145	        // without parentheses; resolve it to the (already-expanded) body. A
146	        // fresh/pub-sorted name or the name of an arity>=1 macro is an ordinary
147	        // variable and is left untouched [Q32,Q33,Q34,Q35]. A nullary macro
148	        // reserves its name even against a same-named formal [Q36].
149	        Term::Var(v) => match table.get(&v.name) {
150	            Some((formals, body)) if v.sort == SortHint::Untagged && formals.is_empty() => {
151	                body.clone()
152	            }
153	            _ => t.clone(),
154	        },
155	
156	        Term::PubLit(_)
157	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
158	        | Term::NatLit(_)
159	        | Term::Number(_)
160	        | Term::NumberOne
161	        | Term::NatOne
162	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
163	
164	        Term::App(name, args) => {
165	            let args: Vec<Term> = args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect();
166	            if let Some((formals, body)) = table.get(name) {
167	                // At the AST level arg-count == arity for a valid parse. If it
168	                // ever differs (defensive), leave the call unexpanded.
169	                if formals.len() == args.len() {
170	                    let subst = build_subst(formals, &args);
171	                    // `body` is already macro-free; a simultaneous substitution
172	                    // of macro-free args therefore yields a macro-free term.
173	                    return substitute_term(body, &subst);
174	                }
175	            }
176	            Term::App(name.clone(), args)
177	        }
178	
179	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
180	            name.clone(),
181	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
182	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
183	        ),
184	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| expand_term(x, table)).collect()),
185	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
186	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
187	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
188	        ),
189	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
190	            *op,
191	            Box::new(expand_term(a, table)),
192	            Box::new(expand_term(b, table)),
193	        ),
194	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(expand_term(a, table))),
195	    }
196	}
197	
198	/// Build the simultaneous substitution `formal_i := arg_i`, keyed by the full
199	/// `VarSpec` of each formal (so sort/idx participate in matching) [Q27,Q28].
200	fn build_subst(formals: &[VarSpec], args: &[Term]) -> HashMap<VarSpec, Term> {
201	    formals
202	        .iter()
203	        .cloned()
204	        .zip(args.iter().cloned())
205	        .collect()
206	}
207	
208	/// Apply a simultaneous substitution to `t`. A `Var` that matches a key is
209	/// replaced by the bound term **without** re-descending into it (parallel
210	/// substitution — no capture) [Q7]. Non-matching variables (incl. differently
211	/// sorted `~x`/`$x`) are left untouched [Q27,Q28].
212	fn substitute_term(t: &Term, subst: &HashMap<VarSpec, Term>) -> Term {
213	    match t {
214	        Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(v) {
215	            Some(bound) => bound.clone(),
216	            None => t.clone(),
217	        },
218	        Term::PubLit(_)
219	        | Term::FreshLit(_)
220	        | Term::NatLit(_)
221	        | Term::Number(_)
222	        | Term::NumberOne
223	        | Term::NatOne
224	        | Term::DhNeutral => t.clone(),
225	        Term::App(name, args) => Term::App(
226	            name.clone(),
227	            args.iter().map(|a| substitute_term(a, subst)).collect(),
228	        ),
229	        Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => Term::AlgApp(
230	            name.clone(),
231	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
232	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
233	        ),
234	        Term::Pair(ts) => Term::Pair(ts.iter().map(|x| substitute_term(x, subst)).collect()),
235	        Term::Diff(a, b) => Term::Diff(
236	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
237	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
238	        ),
239	        Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => Term::BinOp(
240	            *op,
241	            Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst)),
242	            Box::new(substitute_term(b, subst)),
243	        ),
244	        Term::PatMatch(a) => Term::PatMatch(Box::new(substitute_term(a, subst))),
245	    }
246	}
247	
248	// ---- structural recursion through the theory ------------------------------
249	
250	fn expand_fact(f: &Fact, table: &MacroTable) -> Fact {
251	    Fact {
252	        persistent: f.persistent,
253	        name: f.name.clone(),
254	        args: f.args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
255	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
256	    }
257	}
258	
259	fn expand_atom(a: &Atom, table: &MacroTable) -> Atom {
260	    match a {
261	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => Atom::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
262	        Atom::Less(x, y) => Atom::Less(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
263	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => Atom::LessMset(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
264	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => Atom::Subterm(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
265	        Atom::Action(f, t) => Atom::Action(expand_fact(f, table), expand_term(t, table)),
266	        Atom::Last(t) => Atom::Last(expand_term(t, table)),
267	        Atom::Pred(f) => Atom::Pred(expand_fact(f, table)),
268	    }
269	}
270	
271	fn expand_formula(phi: &Formula, table: &MacroTable) -> Formula {
272	    match phi {
273	        Formula::False => Formula::False,
274	        Formula::True => Formula::True,
275	        Formula::Atom(a) => Formula::Atom(expand_atom(a, table)),
276	        Formula::Not(p) => Formula::Not(Box::new(expand_formula(p, table))),
277	        Formula::And(p, q) => Formula::And(
278	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
279	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
280	        ),
281	        Formula::Or(p, q) => Formula::Or(
282	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
283	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
284	        ),
285	        Formula::Implies(p, q) => Formula::Implies(
286	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
287	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
288	        ),
289	        Formula::Iff(p, q) => Formula::Iff(
290	            Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)),
291	            Box::new(expand_formula(q, table)),
292	        ),
293	        Formula::Forall(vs, p) => {
294	            Formula::Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
295	        }
296	        Formula::Exists(vs, p) => {
297	            Formula::Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(expand_formula(p, table)))
298	        }
299	    }
300	}
301	
302	fn expand_let(b: &LetBinding, table: &MacroTable) -> LetBinding {
303	    // A macro call may appear in the let value [Q23]; the bound pattern `var`
304	    // is a variable/pattern and is expanded too for uniformity.
305	    LetBinding {
306	        var: expand_term(&b.var, table),
307	        value: expand_term(&b.value, table),
308	    }
309	}
310	
311	fn expand_rule(r: &Rule, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> Rule {
312	    Rule {
313	        name: r.name.clone(),
314	        modulo: r.modulo.clone(),
315	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
316	        let_block: r.let_block.iter().map(|b| expand_let(b, table)).collect(),
317	        premises: r.premises.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
318	        actions: r.actions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
319	        conclusions: r.conclusions.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
320	        embedded_restrictions: r
321	            .embedded_restrictions
322	            .iter()
323	            .map(|p| expand_formula(p, table))
324	            .collect(),
325	        // Derived rule forms. Full close expands them — the `(modulo AC)` variant
326	        // and left/right diff projections show the expansion [Q3,Q4,Q26]. In the
327	        // staged view a rule exists only in its primary form [Q42], so these are
328	        // carried through untouched.
329	        variants: match mode {
330	            Mode::FullClose => r.variants.iter().map(|v| expand_rule(v, table, mode)).collect(),
331	            Mode::Staged => r.variants.clone(),
332	        },
333	        left_right: match mode {
334	            Mode::FullClose => r.left_right.as_ref().map(|(l, rr)| {
335	                (
336	                    Box::new(expand_rule(l, table, mode)),
337	                    Box::new(expand_rule(rr, table, mode)),
338	                )
339	            }),
340	            Mode::Staged => r.left_right.clone(),
341	        },
342	    }
343	}
344	
345	fn expand_sapic_action(a: &SapicAction, table: &MacroTable) -> SapicAction {
346	    match a {
347	        SapicAction::New(v) => SapicAction::New(v.clone()),
348	        SapicAction::Insert(k, v) => {
349	            SapicAction::Insert(expand_term(k, table), expand_term(v, table))
350	        }
351	        SapicAction::Delete(k) => SapicAction::Delete(expand_term(k, table)),
352	        SapicAction::ChIn { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChIn {
353	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
354	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
355	        },
356	        SapicAction::ChOut { chan, msg } => SapicAction::ChOut {
357	            chan: chan.as_ref().map(|c| expand_term(c, table)),
358	            msg: expand_term(msg, table),
359	        },
360	        SapicAction::Lock(t) => SapicAction::Lock(expand_term(t, table)),
361	        SapicAction::Unlock(t) => SapicAction::Unlock(expand_term(t, table)),
362	        SapicAction::Event(f) => SapicAction::Event(expand_fact(f, table)),
363	        SapicAction::Msr { prems, acts, concs, restrictions } => SapicAction::Msr {
364	            prems: prems.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
365	            acts: acts.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
366	            concs: concs.iter().map(|f| expand_fact(f, table)).collect(),
367	            restrictions: restrictions.iter().map(|p| expand_formula(p, table)).collect(),
368	        },
369	    }
370	}
371	
372	fn expand_condition(c: &Condition, table: &MacroTable) -> Condition {
373	    match c {
374	        Condition::Eq(x, y) => Condition::Eq(expand_term(x, table), expand_term(y, table)),
375	        Condition::Formula(p) => Condition::Formula(expand_formula(p, table)),
376	    }
377	}
378	
379	fn expand_comb(c: &ProcessComb, table: &MacroTable) -> ProcessComb {
380	    match c {
381	        ProcessComb::Parallel => ProcessComb::Parallel,
382	        ProcessComb::Ndc => ProcessComb::Ndc,
383	        ProcessComb::Cond(cond) => ProcessComb::Cond(expand_condition(cond, table)),
384	        ProcessComb::Lookup(t, v) => ProcessComb::Lookup(expand_term(t, table), v.clone()),
385	        ProcessComb::Let { pat, value } => ProcessComb::Let {
386	            pat: expand_term(pat, table),
387	            value: expand_term(value, table),
388	        },
389	    }
390	}
391	
392	fn expand_process(p: &Process, table: &MacroTable) -> Process {
393	    match p {
394	        Process::Null => Process::Null,
395	        Process::Action { action, body } => Process::Action {
396	            action: expand_sapic_action(action, table),
397	            body: Box::new(expand_process(body, table)),
398	        },
399	        Process::Comb { comb, left, right } => Process::Comb {
400	            comb: expand_comb(comb, table),
401	            left: Box::new(expand_process(left, table)),
402	            right: Box::new(expand_process(right, table)),
403	        },
404	        Process::Replication(b) => Process::Replication(Box::new(expand_process(b, table))),
405	        Process::Call { name, args } => Process::Call {
406	            name: name.clone(),
407	            args: args.iter().map(|a| expand_term(a, table)).collect(),
408	        },
409	        Process::AtAnnotation(b, t) => {
410	            Process::AtAnnotation(Box::new(expand_process(b, table)), expand_term(t, table))
411	        }
412	    }
413	}
414	
415	fn expand_restriction(r: &Restriction, table: &MacroTable) -> Restriction {
416	    Restriction {
417	        name: r.name.clone(),
418	        formula: expand_formula(&r.formula, table),
419	        attributes: r.attributes.clone(),
420	    }
421	}
422	
423	fn expand_lemma(l: &Lemma, table: &MacroTable) -> Lemma {
424	    Lemma {
425	        name: l.name.clone(),
426	        modulo: l.modulo.clone(),
427	        attributes: l.attributes.clone(),
428	        trace_quantifier: l.trace_quantifier.clone(),
429	        formula: expand_formula(&l.formula, table),
430	        proof: l.proof.clone(),
431	        plaintext: l.plaintext.clone(),
432	    }
433	}
434	
435	fn expand_predicate(p: &Predicate, table: &MacroTable) -> Predicate {
436	    Predicate {
437	        fact: expand_fact(&p.fact, table),
438	        formula: expand_formula(&p.formula, table),
439	    }
440	}
441	
442	fn expand_item(it: &TheoryItem, table: &MacroTable, mode: Mode) -> TheoryItem {
443	    match it {
444	        TheoryItem::Rule(r) => TheoryItem::Rule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
445	        TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => TheoryItem::IntrRule(expand_rule(r, table, mode)),
446	        TheoryItem::Restriction(r) => TheoryItem::Restriction(expand_restriction(r, table)),
447	        TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(expand_restriction(r, table)),
448	        TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => TheoryItem::Lemma(expand_lemma(l, table)),
449	        // acc-lemma & case-test formulas: full close expands them (the generated
450	        // lemmas' guarded forms show the expansion [Q38,Q39]); the staged view
451	        // leaves them untouched because a later consumer stage owns them [Q41].
452	        TheoryItem::AccLemma(a) => match mode {
453	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::AccLemma(AccLemma {
454	                name: a.name.clone(),
455	                attributes: a.attributes.clone(),
456	                formula: expand_formula(&a.formula, table),
457	                case_test_idents: a.case_test_idents.clone(),
458	            }),
459	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
460	        },
461	        TheoryItem::CaseTest(c) => match mode {
462	            Mode::FullClose => TheoryItem::CaseTest(CaseTest {
463	                name: c.name.clone(),
464	                formula: expand_formula(&c.formula, table),
465	            }),
466	            Mode::Staged => it.clone(),
467	        },
468	        TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
469	            TheoryItem::Predicates(ps.iter().map(|p| expand_predicate(p, table)).collect())
470	        }
471	        TheoryItem::ProcessDef(pd) => TheoryItem::ProcessDef(ProcessDef {
472	            name: pd.name.clone(),
473	            vars: pd.vars.clone(),
474	            body: expand_process(&pd.body, table),
475	        }),
476	        TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(p) => {
477	            TheoryItem::TopLevelProcess(expand_process(p, table))
478	        }
479	        TheoryItem::EquivLemma(a, b) => {
480	            TheoryItem::EquivLemma(expand_process(a, table), expand_process(b, table))
481	        }
482	        TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(p) => {
483	            TheoryItem::DiffEquivLemma(expand_process(p, table))
484	        }
485	        // Equations carry terms; expand uniformly (a no-op when they contain no
486	        // macro call — not observed in the corpus, but handled for robustness).
487	        TheoryItem::Equations { convergent, eqs } => TheoryItem::Equations {
488	            convergent: *convergent,
489	            eqs: eqs
490	                .iter()
491	                .map(|e| Equation {
492	                    lhs: expand_term(&e.lhs, table),
493	                    rhs: expand_term(&e.rhs, table),
494	                })
495	                .collect(),
496	        },
497	        // Items with no macro-bearing terms are carried through unchanged. The
498	        // `macros:` declaration items pass through here too — retained in place
499	        // with their original, unexpanded bodies [Q37].
500	        TheoryItem::Macros(_)
501	        | TheoryItem::Builtins(_)
502	        | TheoryItem::Functions(_)
503	        | TheoryItem::Options(_)
504	        | TheoryItem::Heuristic(_)
505	        | TheoryItem::Tactic(_)
506	        | TheoryItem::DiffLemma(_)
507	        | TheoryItem::Export { .. }
508	        | TheoryItem::FormalComment { .. }
509	        | TheoryItem::Define(_)
510	        | TheoryItem::Include(_) => it.clone(),
511	    }
512	}

5	//   other minor contributors (see upstream git history)
6	// Ported from upstream tamarin-prover sources:
7	//   lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs, lib/theory/src/ClosedTheory.hs,
8	//   lib/theory/src/Items/CaseTestItem.hs, lib/theory/src/Lemma.hs,
9	//   lib/theory/src/Prover.hs, lib/theory/src/Rule.hs,
10	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Fact.hs,
11	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Formula.hs,
12	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Restriction.hs,
13	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Rule.hs,
14	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Sapic/Term.hs,
15	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser.hs,
16	//   lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser/Macro.hs
17	
18	//! Parser-AST level macro expansion.
19	//!
20	//! Port of `Term.Macro.applyMacros` (HS: lib/term/src/Term/Macro.hs:40-50)
21	//! plus the call-sites that drive it:
22	//!
23	//!   - `applyMacroInRule`     — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Rule.hs:1032-1037
24	//!   - `applyMacroInFact`     — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Fact.hs:301-303
25	//!   - `applyMacroInFormula`  — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Formula.hs:311-313
26	//!   - `applyMacroInLemma`    — lib/theory/src/Lemma.hs:83-88
27	//!   - `applyMacroInRestriction` — lib/theory/src/Theory/Model/Restriction.hs:163-165
28	//!   - `closeProtoRule` calls applyMacroInRule BEFORE variantsProtoRule
29	//!     — lib/theory/src/Rule.hs:96-98
30	//!   - `parseLemmaWithMacros`  — lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Parser.hs:97-105
31	//!
32	//! HS works at the typed `LNTerm` / `LNFact` / `LNFormula` level, with
33	//! macro matching keyed on the `FunSym` (a `NoEq (name, (arity, Private,
34	//! Destructor))` tuple — Macro.hs:30).  RS parses lemma/restriction
35	//! formulas as `parser::ast::Formula` and only converts to `LNFormula`
36	//! later (via `formula_to_guarded`), so the natural place to expand is
37	//! the parser AST.  This is observationally faithful: every macro call
38	//! site is rewritten to its body before either side's typed conversion
39	//! runs.  The macro fun-syms themselves are still registered in MaudeSig
40	//! (HS Parser/Macro.hs:48 `addMacroSym`) so any unexpanded reference —
41	//! and Maude — still see them.
42	//!
43	//! Recursion semantics mirror HS exactly:
44	//!   - args are recursively expanded FIRST (Macro.hs:46),
45	//!   - then substitution into the body,
46	//!   - then the EXPANDED body is recursively re-expanded
47	//!     (Macro.hs:48 `applyMacros macros (apply subst mout)`).
48	//!
49	//! This handles chained / nested macros (e.g. `hashdec` calling `decrypt`
50	//! in `examples/features/macros/MacroExample.spthy`).
51	
52	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
53	
54	use tamarin_parser::ast as p;
55	
56	/// Apply all macros to a term, recursing into args first and re-expanding
57	/// the body after substitution.  Mirrors HS `applyMacros` exactly
58	/// (Term/Macro.hs:40-50).
59	pub fn apply_macros_term(macros: &[p::Macro], term: &p::Term) -> p::Term {
60	    match term {
61	        p::Term::App(name, args) => {
62	            // Recurse on args first (HS `processedArgs = map (applyMacros macros) args`).
63	            let processed_args: Vec<p::Term> =
64	                args.iter().map(|a| apply_macros_term(macros, a)).collect();
65	            // Match on (name, arity) — HS matches on FunSym which includes
66	            // arity (Macro.hs:30 `macroToFunSym (op,args,_) = NoEq (op,
67	            // (length args, Private, Destructor))` ; matching macros are
68	            // found via Macro.hs:53-54 `find (\m -> macroToFunSym m == f)`).
69	            if let Some(m) = find_matching_macro(name, processed_args.len(), macros) {
70	                // Build the param→arg substitution (by name).
71	                let mut subst: BTreeMap<String, p::Term> = BTreeMap::new();
72	                for (param, value) in m.args.iter().zip(processed_args.iter()) {
73	                    subst.insert(param.name.clone(), value.clone());
74	                }
75	                let expanded = subst_term_by_name(&m.body, &subst);
76	                // Re-expand the EXPANDED body to handle nested macros.
77	                apply_macros_term(macros, &expanded)
78	            } else {
79	                p::Term::App(name.clone(), processed_args)
80	            }
81	        }
82	        p::Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => p::Term::AlgApp(
83	            name.clone(),
84	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
85	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
86	        ),
87	        p::Term::Pair(items) => p::Term::Pair(
88	            items.iter().map(|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t)).collect(),
89	        ),
90	        p::Term::Diff(a, b) => p::Term::Diff(
91	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
92	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
93	        ),
94	        p::Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => p::Term::BinOp(
95	            *op,
96	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, a)),
97	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, b)),
98	        ),
99	        p::Term::PatMatch(inner) => p::Term::PatMatch(
100	            Box::new(apply_macros_term(macros, inner)),
101	        ),
102	        // A BARE identifier (no `$~#%` prefix, no `:sort` suffix, no `.idx`)
103	        // naming a 0-ary macro is a macro CALL: HS's `nullaryApp` parser
104	        // alternative (Term.hs:143-148) runs before `plit` and matches any
105	        // arity-0 name in `funSyms ∪ macroNames`, so such an identifier
106	        // reaches HS's `applyMacros` as `fApp (NoEq (m,(0,..))) []`, never
107	        // as a variable.  RS's surface parser is signature-less and yields
108	        // `Var`, so resolve here.  Any sort/index decoration means HS's
109	        // `symbol` match would have left trailing input and backtracked to
110	        // `plit` — a genuine variable; leave those (and 0-ary FUNCTION
111	        // names, which `term_to_lnterm`'s USER_NULLARY_FUNS branch lifts)
112	        // untouched.
113	        p::Term::Var(v) => {
114	            if v.idx == 0 && v.sort == p::SortHint::Untagged && v.typ.is_none() {
115	                if let Some(m) = find_matching_macro(&v.name, 0, macros) {
116	                    let expanded = subst_term_by_name(&m.body, &BTreeMap::new());
117	                    // Re-expand the EXPANDED body to handle nested macros.
118	                    return apply_macros_term(macros, &expanded);
119	                }
120	            }
121	            term.clone()
122	        }
123	        // Literals: no recursion (HS Macro.hs:51 `Lit l -> lit l`).
124	        p::Term::PubLit(_) | p::Term::FreshLit(_)
125	        | p::Term::NatLit(_) | p::Term::Number(_) | p::Term::NumberOne
126	        | p::Term::NatOne | p::Term::DhNeutral => term.clone(),
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	/// HS `findMatchingMacro f macros = find (\m -> macroToFunSym m == f)`
131	/// (Macro.hs:53-54).  At parser-AST level a call-site has no FunSym
132	/// flags so we match by (name, arity) — equivalent for non-clashing
133	/// macro names since `addMacroSym` rejects redefinitions and built-in
134	/// fun-syms have known fixed arity (parser/Macro.hs:45-49 `case lookup
135	/// op (stFunSyms ++ macroNames) -> fail`).
136	fn find_matching_macro<'a>(
137	    name: &str,
138	    arity: usize,
139	    macros: &'a [p::Macro],
140	) -> Option<&'a p::Macro> {
141	    macros.iter().find(|m| m.name == name && m.args.len() == arity)
142	}
143	
144	/// Apply a name-keyed substitution to a parser term.  HS's typed
145	/// `apply subst term` (Macro.hs:48) becomes a structural name-keyed walk.
146	/// Shared with `predicate_expand` (whose `Subst` newtype wraps the same
147	/// `BTreeMap<String, p::Term>`), so both stay in lockstep on capture /
148	/// replacement semantics.
149	pub(crate) fn subst_term_by_name(t: &p::Term, subst: &BTreeMap<String, p::Term>) -> p::Term {
150	    match t {
151	        p::Term::Var(v) => match subst.get(&v.name) {
152	            Some(replacement) => replacement.clone(),
153	            None => t.clone(),
154	        },
155	        p::Term::App(name, args) => p::Term::App(
156	            name.clone(),
157	            args.iter().map(|a| subst_term_by_name(a, subst)).collect(),
158	        ),
159	        p::Term::AlgApp(name, a, b) => p::Term::AlgApp(
160	            name.clone(),
161	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
162	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
163	        ),
164	        p::Term::Pair(items) => p::Term::Pair(
165	            items.iter().map(|a| subst_term_by_name(a, subst)).collect(),
166	        ),
167	        p::Term::Diff(a, b) => p::Term::Diff(
168	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
169	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
170	        ),
171	        p::Term::BinOp(op, a, b) => p::Term::BinOp(
172	            *op,
173	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(a, subst)),
174	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(b, subst)),
175	        ),
176	        p::Term::PatMatch(inner) => p::Term::PatMatch(
177	            Box::new(subst_term_by_name(inner, subst)),
178	        ),
179	        other => other.clone(),
180	    }

183	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild a fact, mapping `g` over every arg.
184	/// The single traversal shape behind `apply_macros_fact`,
185	/// `elaborate::canonicalize_ac_in_pfact`, and `elaborate::rewrite_arity1_fact`
186	/// (each supplies its own leaf `&Term -> Term`).
187	pub(crate) fn map_fact_terms(f: &p::Fact, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Fact {
188	    p::Fact {
189	        persistent: f.persistent,
190	        name: f.name.clone(),
191	        args: f.args.iter().map(g).collect(),
192	        annotations: f.annotations.clone(),
193	    }
194	}
195	
196	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild an atom, mapping `g` over every term and
197	/// `map_fact_terms` over embedded facts.  See [`map_fact_terms`].
198	pub(crate) fn map_atom_terms(a: &p::Atom, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Atom {
199	    use p::Atom::*;
200	    match a {
201	        Eq(x, y) => Eq(g(x), g(y)),
202	        Less(x, y) => Less(g(x), g(y)),
203	        LessMset(x, y) => LessMset(g(x), g(y)),
204	        Subterm(x, y) => Subterm(g(x), g(y)),
205	        Action(f, t) => Action(map_fact_terms(f, g), g(t)),
206	        Last(t) => Last(g(t)),
207	        Pred(f) => Pred(map_fact_terms(f, g)),
208	    }
209	}
210	
211	/// Shared structural walker: rebuild a formula, mapping `g` over every leaf
212	/// term while cloning quantifier `VarSpec`s unchanged.  See [`map_fact_terms`].
213	pub(crate) fn map_formula_terms(f: &p::Formula, g: &dyn Fn(&p::Term) -> p::Term) -> p::Formula {
214	    use p::Formula::*;
215	    match f {
216	        False => False,
217	        True => True,
218	        Atom(a) => Atom(map_atom_terms(a, g)),
219	        Not(x) => Not(Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
220	        And(x, y) => And(
221	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
222	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
223	        Or(x, y) => Or(
224	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
225	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
226	        Implies(x, y) => Implies(
227	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
228	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
229	        Iff(x, y) => Iff(
230	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g)),
231	            Box::new(map_formula_terms(y, g))),
232	        Forall(vs, x) => Forall(vs.clone(), Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
233	        Exists(vs, x) => Exists(vs.clone(), Box::new(map_formula_terms(x, g))),
234	    }
235	}
