237	/// Apply macros to every term in a fact.  Mirrors HS `applyMacroInFact`
238	/// (Fact.hs:301-303 `applyMacroInFact mcs (Fact tag annot terms) =
239	/// Fact tag annot (map (applyMacros mcs) terms)`).
240	pub fn apply_macros_fact(macros: &[p::Macro], f: &p::Fact) -> p::Fact {
241	    map_fact_terms(f, &|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t))
242	}
243	
244	/// Apply macros to every term in a formula.  Mirrors HS
245	/// `applyMacroInFormula` (Formula.hs:311-313) — `mapAtoms (... applyMacros
246	/// (lnMacrosToBNMacros macros))`.  In RS, parser-AST quantifiers carry
247	/// `VarSpec`s with names; macro params have their declared names; the
248	/// substitution-by-name suffices because the body is closed over the
249	/// param names and call args at every call site, so no quantifier-bound
250	/// variable in the surrounding formula can ever be a param (the macro
251	/// definition is independent of the use site).
252	pub fn apply_macros_formula(macros: &[p::Macro], f: &p::Formula) -> p::Formula {
253	    map_formula_terms(f, &|t| apply_macros_term(macros, t))
254	}
255	
256	/// Apply macros to all items in a theory.  Mirrors HS's call-sites:
257	///   - rule prems/concs/acts (Rule.hs:1032-1037 + ClosedTheory.hs:322-323)
258	///   - lemma formula (Lemma.hs:83-88, called from Parser.hs:105)
259	///   - restriction formula (Restriction.hs:163-165)
260	///   - embedded restriction in rule (treat as formula)
261	///   - rule let-block RHS (already inlined into the rule by
262	///     `apply_let_block` at elaborate time)
263	///
264	/// Macros are collected from `TheoryItem::Macros` items in the theory.
265	/// If no macros are declared, the theory is left unchanged (HS:
266	/// `applyMacroInFormula [] fm = fm`).
267	pub fn expand_theory_macros(thy: &mut p::Theory) {
268	    let macros: Vec<p::Macro> = thy.items.iter().filter_map(|i| match i {
269	        p::TheoryItem::Macros(ms) => Some(ms.clone()),
270	        _ => None,
271	    }).flatten().collect();
272	
273	    if macros.is_empty() { return; }
274	
275	    expand_items(&macros, &mut thy.items);
276	}
277	
278	/// Clone a parser theory and expand its macros, mirroring HS `thyProtoRules`'s
279	/// `applyMacroInRule (theoryMacros thy)`.  Used for the WF re-checks (batch and
280	/// web load paths) that must see macro-expanded rules.
281	pub fn macro_expanded_clone(parsed: &p::Theory) -> p::Theory {
282	    let mut t = parsed.clone();
283	    expand_theory_macros(&mut t);
284	    t
285	}
286	
287	/// Apply macros to a slice of theory items.  The parser splices `#ifdef`
288	/// live branches into the top-level stream (like HS's parse-time
289	/// preprocessing), so a plain walk sees every macro call-site.
290	fn expand_items(macros: &[p::Macro], items: &mut [p::TheoryItem]) {
291	    for item in items.iter_mut() {
292	        match item {
293	            p::TheoryItem::Rule(r) | p::TheoryItem::IntrRule(r) => {
294	                expand_rule(macros, r);
295	            }
296	            p::TheoryItem::Lemma(l) => {
297	                l.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &l.formula);
298	            }
299	            p::TheoryItem::Restriction(r) | p::TheoryItem::LegacyAxiom(r) => {
300	                r.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &r.formula);
301	            }
302	            // CaseTest / AccLemma are `TranslationItem`s in HS, which
303	            // `closeTheoryItem` passes through verbatim with NO macro
304	            // application (Prover.hs:204 `TranslationItem`; added unmacroed
305	            // via Parser.hs:157,163 `liftedAddAccLemma`/`liftedAddCaseTest`).
306	            // They stay `SyntacticLNFormula` and are only `toLNFormula`'d
307	            // during accountability translation (Items/CaseTestItem.hs:34-37),
308	            // which does not run macros. So we deliberately do NOT expand them
309	            // (they fall into the `_ => {}` arm below).
310	            //
311	            // Predicates: bodies are themselves formula templates. Apply
312	            // macros so a predicate body that calls a macro is expanded
313	            // before predicate-expand inlines it.
314	            p::TheoryItem::Predicates(ps) => {
315	                for pred in ps.iter_mut() {
316	                    pred.formula = apply_macros_formula(macros, &pred.formula);
317	                    pred.fact = apply_macros_fact(macros, &pred.fact);
318	                }
319	            }
320	            _ => {}
321	        }
322	    }
323	}
324	
325	fn expand_rule(macros: &[p::Macro], r: &mut p::Rule) {
326	    for f in &mut r.premises { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
327	    for f in &mut r.actions { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
328	    for f in &mut r.conclusions { *f = apply_macros_fact(macros, f); }
329	    for phi in &mut r.embedded_restrictions {
330	        *phi = apply_macros_formula(macros, phi);
331	    }
332	    // Let-block: macros can appear on the RHS.  apply_let_block (in
333	    // elaborate.rs) substitutes these into the body after parsing; we
334	    // expand on the LHS-and-RHS terms here so a let `x = macro(...)`
335	    // sees its RHS rewritten before `apply_let_block` substitutes it.
336	    for b in &mut r.let_block {
337	        b.value = apply_macros_term(macros, &b.value);
338	        b.var = apply_macros_term(macros, &b.var);
339	    }
340	    // Variants / diff sides are passed through UNCHANGED, matching HS.
341	    // `variants` is the user-written explicit `variants ...` block (HS
342	    // OpenProtoRule's ruAC) and `left_right` is the diff `left ... right ...`
343	    // block (HS DiffProtoRule's sides). `applyMacroInProtoRule` /
344	    // `applyMacroInDiffProtoRule` (ClosedTheory.hs:319,323) only run
345	    // applyMacroInRule on the main rule `ruE` and leave variants/sides intact,
346	    // so a macro call inside an explicit variant must survive unexpanded.
347	}

# Split-stream probe: runs the reference binary capturing stdout and stderr
# separately into captures/<label>.out.txt and captures/<label>.err.txt, and
# prints the exit code. Usage: split_probe.sh <label> [args...]
# The binary path mirrors oracle/hs_oracle.sh (compiled black-box binary).

BIN="/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover"

echo "--- $label.out.txt ($(wc -c <"$CAP/$label.out.txt") bytes) ---"

echo "--- $label.err.txt ($(wc -c <"$CAP/$label.err.txt") bytes) ---"

1	//! Output-stream model.
2	//!
3	//! Every byte the binary emits is destined for exactly one of the two standard
4	//! streams. Which stream a given piece of text lands on is a fixed, probed
5	//! property (see `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` §10): the theory payload, the summary
6	//! block, help pages, the version banner, and the `error: … + full help`
7	//! validation envelopes go to stdout; the maude preamble, per-theory progress
8	//! markers, the bare cmdargs one-liners, and all runtime errors go to stderr.

10	/// One of the two standard output streams.
11	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
12	pub enum Stream {
13	    /// Standard output.
14	    Out,
15	    /// Standard error.
16	    Err,
17	}
18	
19	/// A pair of independently byte-exact stream buffers.
20	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
21	pub struct Streams {
22	    pub out: String,
23	    pub err: String,
24	}
25

27	    /// Append `text` to the buffer for `stream`.
28	    pub fn push(&mut self, stream: Stream, text: &str) {
29	        match stream {
30	            Stream::Out => self.out.push_str(text),

Wave-2 integration complete. Tree is building and green; no git commits made.

## Unit E (macros) — SWAPPED + PORTED DRIVER DELETED, byte-verified

- Re-synced `crates/tamarin-theory/src/macros.rs` from the round-6 workspace: the `expand_term` `Var` arm now guards on full non-decoration (`formals.is_empty() && sort==Untagged && idx==0 && typ.is_none()`) — the exact gap that failed `bare_nullary_macro_name_expands` last round. Stays headerless.
- Rewired `macro_expand::expand_theory_macros` / `macro_expanded_clone` through `macros::expand_staged`, then **deleted** the ported `expand_theory_macros` body, `expand_items`, and `expand_rule`. Kept (header intact) the shared, still-required machinery: `apply_macros_term`/`_fact`/`_formula`, `subst_term_by_name`, `map_{fact,atom,formula}_terms` (used by `pretty_theory`, `predicate_expand`, `elaborate`, `rule_restriction`), plus the two thin routing entry points. Module doc rewritten to current state.
- Gate green: all **9** `macro_expand` captured tests pass UNMODIFIED (incl. `bare_nullary_macro_name_expands`, acc-lemma/case-test non-expansion); theory suite 489/19/5; wf fixtures 2/2. Live end-to-end: a macro theory (bare-nullary + multi-arg + nested/pair) under `--prove=reachable` is **byte-identical to the v1.13.0 HS binary** (modulo build metadata).

## Unit D (console) — round-5 RE-SYNC landed green; run-driver swap KEPT

- Vendored the round-5 clean delta: `cli/framing.rs` (summary-body content — `LemmaOutcome`, `WarningSummary` with the `--prove` advisory, `Summary.{warnings,lemmas}`) and NEW `cli/emit.rs` (incremental `Sink`/`BatchEmitter`/`drive_batch`), both headerless; registered `pub mod emit;`. Re-ported the round-5 test delta + 8 `r5_*` golden fixtures: `console_split_parity` **33 → 45**, exercising the whole new surface.
- The run-driver contract swap is **KEPT ported** (never-force-a-swap), blocked by two now-concrete gaps confirmed with live oracle captures:
  1. **Clean-side verdict-taxonomy gap** — clean `LemmaResult` models 3 verdicts; `run.rs` renders 9 (`Unfinishable`/`Undetermined`/`Invalidated`/`Error` have distinct HS `showProofStatus` strings, pinned by a `run.rs` unit test). Routing the summary through the clean emitter is a *silent* byte regression no green gate catches → the clean crate must add those verdicts.
  2. **Ported-scale `Args` re-wire** — `run.rs` consumes ~40 ported `Args` fields (incl. the 3 Rust-only interop flags); deleting `parse_args`/`Args` = rewiring the 1800-line driver, un-validatable without the full corpus gate; `cli_e2e` pins the contract it would change. Blockers 1–3 (validation-ordering, structural-taxonomy, stop-on-trace) re-captured live (`--bound=x` preamble-then-error; `--foobar` 23B stderr/0 stdout; no-input 8625B stdout envelope).

## Headers / citations
- Count **133 → 133** (no headered file added or deleted; no clean file acquired a header — `macros.rs`, `cli/framing.rs`, `cli/emit.rs` verified headerless; no provenance violation). `--check`: 0 stale.
- `gen_license_headers.py` re-derived `crates/tamarin-theory/src/macro_expand.rs`'s header from the smaller ported content, dropping `gilcu3` and `katrielalex` from **that file's** header (the deleted drivers they authored) — **both survive campaign-wide** (~10 other headered files each). Cosmetic side effect: the tool canonicalized `crates/tamarin-term/src/macro_expand.rs`'s author line (`"Tom" (github BTom-GH), "ValentinYuri" (github)` → `ValentinYuri, BTom-GH`) — same authors.
